title: Customer Journey Map Visual Renderer — Slice 1 (Render-First)
mission_summary: Build an interactive, zero-dependency HTML renderer for the existing customer_journey_map.schema.json that produces a beautiful, brand-compliant journey map a PM can open in a browser, iterate on, and share. This is the render-first slice: the JSON is manually authored or pre-existing; synthesis from persona/research artifacts comes in Slice 2.
target_user: Product Manager using ProductOS to map and communicate customer journeys
customer_problem: PMs have journey map data in structured JSON but cannot turn it into a visual artifact they can present, iterate on, or share with stakeholders. Existing tools require manual design work or export to PowerPoint which breaks interactivity.
business_goal: Give PMs a one-command path from structured journey data to a consultant-grade interactive visual that influences teams and stakeholders.
primary_outcomes: Standalone interactive HTML journey map from customer_journey_map.json
Visual regression test coverage for stage count, emotion curve, pain-point badges, and interactivity
primary_kpis: Render completes in <2 seconds for 11-stage journey
Zero external runtime dependencies (embedded CSS + vanilla JS)
success_metrics: HTML contains all 11 stages from example artifact with correct emotion color coding
SVG emotion curve renders with peak at adoption (score 9) and valley at purchase (score 5)
Clicking a stage opens detail panel with persona actions, thoughts, touchpoints, channels, pain points, drop-off risk
constraints: Use ProductOS brand tokens from design_token_set.example.json (Inter, #2563EB primary, #DC2626 error, #D97706 warning, #16A34A success)
Keep the slice bounded: no synthesis runtime, no living system integration, no deck export in this slice

mission_router.routing_rationale: Render-first slice: validate the visual language and interactivity with existing JSON data before investing in synthesis runtime. Align phase validates the output is presentation-ready.
stop_conditions: Stop if visual output does not match ProductOS brand tokens and style system
Stop if interactivity requires external dependencies
Stop if test coverage is below the bounded claim

operating_norms: Treat the repository as the system of record
Keep PM approval explicit for decision-driving scope and release movement
Prefer the smallest coherent slice that can be reviewed and validated end to end
Render-first: get the visual right, then automate the data pipeline

stage_goals.discovery: Validate the customer_journey_map schema supports the visual features needed (11 stages, emotion scores, touchpoints, pain points, moments of truth, opportunities)
stage_goals.align: Produce a standalone HTML renderer that passes visual regression tests and is ready for PM iteration and stakeholder presentation

known_risks: 11 stages may be too wide for standard browser viewport — need horizontal scroll or responsive collapse
Emotion curve SVG math must be precise to avoid visual glitches across stage widths
Slice 2 (synthesis runtime) is blocked until this render slice is validated
next_action: Create components/journey_engine/ directory, implement journey_visual_renderer.py, wire CLI command, add tests, validate against example artifact
